mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and impending recession risk, which triggers earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in post-2008 data.

mechanism_narrative: Stagnant real GDP growth combined with elevated consumer price inflation creates a stagflationary environment that erodes real corporate margins and forces prolonged monetary tightening, depressing equity valuations.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when GDP growth is below 1.5% and CPI exceeds 3.5%, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates successful supply chain normalization and expanding capacity utilization, which directly supports cyclical sector earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Industrial production rises while S&P 500 forward returns remain negative for more than 12 consecutive months, indicating a decoupling of supply metrics from equity valuations.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.2 million units signals a sharp contraction in residential investment and broader consumer confidence, historically leading to negative equity returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: The housing threshold breach consistently coincides with positive S&P 500 forward returns, or the predictive power vanishes after adjusting for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong payroll growth and a rising unemployment rate indicates structural labor market distortion and wage-price spiral risks, which increases earnings volatility and drives negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative returns when payroll growth exceeds 200k and unemployment rises simultaneously, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales and consumer spending directly drive corporate revenue growth, positively impacting S&P 500 earnings expectations and generating positive forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are not significantly higher when retail sales growth exceeds 2% month-over-month, or the signal shows negative predictive power in high-inflation environments.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, the joint persistence of high federal funds rates and sticky inflation forces a policy error risk that compresses equity risk premiums and drives negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint high-rate/high-inflation regime fails to predict negative returns, or S&P 500 forward returns remain positive despite both series staying at current elevated levels for two consecutive quarters.
